paper_title: Materials and Methods of Pleistocene Branchiopod Study

methods.description: Process for examining and preparing fossilized branchiopod samples from Pleistocene localities for further analysis.

steps.description: Fossil remains were placed in a small Petri dish and examined in clean water under a Leica MZ7.5 stereomicroscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Examine fossil remains in clean water using a stereomicroscope

steps.description: All recognizable branchiopod crustacean fragments were collected using a pipette or forceps and transferred to 96% alcohol.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect branchiopod fragments and transfer to alcohol

steps.description: Some branchiopod remains were preliminarily studied with a high power Olympus CX-41 microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Study branchiopod remains with a high power microscope

steps.description: A portion of the branchiopod remains was air dried, attached to stubs, coated with gold in an S150A Sputter Coater, and studied under a Tescan Vega TS5130MM scanning electron microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare and study branchiopod remains with SEM

steps.description: Individual specimens were identified using relevant literature, references collections, and personal experience.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify specimens using literature and reference collections
